markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games confirmed Grand Theft Auto VI's release for November 19, 2026, on PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X/S after two delays from the original Fall 2025 window to prioritize polish. This extended timeline to late 2026 creates a longer window for unrelated cultural and global events to resolve before launch, driving trader focus on high-profile outcomes like geopolitical developments or major music and tech releases. Industry analysts note Rockstar's history of meticulous marketing campaigns, with potential for additional trailers or updates in the coming months to build hype without altering the firm date. The market reflects aggregated sentiment on which milestone crosses the finish line first amid fluctuating real-world news cycles.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games has twice delayed Grand Theft Auto VI from earlier targets, with the most recent official announcement setting the release for November 19, 2026, following an interim May 2026 window that was pushed back in late 2025. Recent Take-Two Interactive earnings calls and industry reporting have reaffirmed this timeline with no indications of further acceleration, reflecting the extensive post-production polish typical for Rockstar's major titles. This established schedule drives the near-certain trader consensus against a pre-June 2026 launch. A surprise early drop remains theoretically possible only if development wrapped far ahead of projections and executives reversed course on quality controls, though historical patterns and current production status make such an outcome highly improbable.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders assign 75.4% implied probability to no additional prison time for Harvey Weinstein primarily due to the May 15, 2026 mistrial in his third New York retrial on the Jessica Mann rape charge, marking the second hung jury in that case. Prosecutors now have until a June 24 hearing to decide on pursuing another retrial, while Weinstein continues appealing his California conviction carrying a consecutive 16-year sentence. His age and documented health concerns at Rikers have further fueled market sentiment that existing convictions may not result in prolonged incarceration. The low probabilities on multi-year outcomes reflect the competitive legal dynamics, where successful appeals or prosecutorial decisions against further proceedings could shift outcomes quickly.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Carolina Hurricanes lead Stanley Cup futures at 48.5% implied probability thanks to their Metropolitan Division title, Eastern Conference regular-season points lead, and strong playoff depth despite splitting the first two games of the Eastern Conference Final with the Canadiens. The Vegas Golden Knights sit at 39.4% after taking early control of the Western Conference Final against the Presidents’ Trophy-winning Avalanche, capitalizing on resilient comebacks and goaltending stability. Montreal’s 7.8% reflects underdog status in a tight series, while Colorado’s 4.5% has dropped amid series deficits and recent losses that shifted market sentiment toward the Knights.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Carolina Hurricanes lead Stanley Cup futures at 48.5% implied probability thanks to their Metropolitan Division title, Eastern Conference regular-season points lead, and strong playoff depth despite splitting the first two games of the Eastern Conference Final with the Canadiens. The Vegas Golden Knights sit at 39.4% after taking early control of the Western Conference Final against the Presidents’ Trophy-winning Avalanche, capitalizing on resilient comebacks and goaltending stability. Montreal’s 7.8% reflects underdog status in a tight series, while Colorado’s 4.5% has dropped amid series deficits and recent losses that shifted market sentiment toward the Knights.

markets.question: Will the Montreal Canadiens win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Montreal Canadiens win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Oklahoma City Thunder hold the highest implied probability in this market due to their established core of young talent, strong regular-season form, and depth that has kept them atop Western Conference standings. The New York Knicks follow at 29.8 percent, driven by consistent Eastern Conference contention, veteran leadership, and defensive identity that has translated into repeated deep playoff runs. San Antonio Spurs sit at 26.5 percent, reflecting continued development around a generational frontcourt presence and improved supporting cast that has elevated them into title contention. Recent roster continuity, injury management, and schedule results over the past month have reinforced these positions among traders assessing championship paths.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France sit atop the 2026 FIFA World Cup winner market at nearly identical implied probabilities because both enter as the standout European sides with recent major-tournament success, elite FIFA rankings, and exceptional squad depth across attack and midfield. Spain’s European Championship pedigree and fluid style contrast with France’s proven knockout experience and athleticism, while England’s consistent deep runs, Portugal’s attacking talent, and the South American duo of defending champions Argentina plus Brazil keep the field bunched. Strong qualification form, minimal disruptive injuries, and a balanced bracket further compress the probabilities among these contenders, underscoring how evenly matched the top tier remains heading into the June kickoff.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping maintains firm control as General Secretary of the Chinese Communist Party and Chairman of the Central Military Commission, with recent anti-corruption campaigns and military leadership changes further centralizing authority without evident elite opposition or designated successor. In early 2026, he directed preparations for the 15th Five-Year Plan covering 2026–2030 and conducted extensive diplomatic engagements across regions, while his New Year address emphasized policy continuity including on Taiwan. These developments, alongside the absence of confirmed health concerns or institutional challenges, align with the 21st Party Congress scheduled for late 2027 as the next potential juncture for leadership adjustments. Trader consensus at 93% against removal before 2027 reflects this ongoing stability and the lack of verifiable transition pressures within the market window.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028

eventMetadata.context_description: The wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential field, with no declared frontrunner after the 2024 cycle, has produced fragmented trader consensus reflected in current market pricing, where Gavin Newsom leads at 24.3% due to his position as California governor and associated national profile. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez follows at 10.1% amid progressive base support and visibility, while Kamala Harris sits at 8.3% drawing on prior national candidacy recognition. Key differentiators include executive governing records versus legislative or media profiles, with early polling averages showing name recognition advantages for several contenders. Upcoming 2026 midterms, potential endorsements, and primary-state positioning could consolidate support, as historical patterns indicate fields often narrow rapidly once active campaigns begin.
